lines.line: # Introduction to the Interview and Brian Mooney's Background

> The interview begins with the state historian, Hannah Laura Hein, introducing herself and the purpose of the oral history project. Brian Mooney, the interviewee, is asked to state his name and spell it out. The speaker then provides a brief overview of his background, including his upbringing in Idaho, education in Wyoming, and professional journey that led him back to Idaho. He discusses his career progression, family life, and his eventual role as the Vice President of Operations at Agri Beef, highlighting the growth and success of the company.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Interview and Brian Mooney's Background

Brian Mooney, the interviewee, is asked to state his name and spell it out. The speaker then provides a brief overview of his background, including his upbringing in Idaho, education in Wyoming, and professional journey that led him back to Idaho. He discusses his career progression, family life, and his eventual role as the Vice President of Operations at Agri Beef, highlighting the growth and success of the company.

lines.line: # Experiences in the Covid-19 Working Group

> The speaker recounts his experiences as a member of Governor Brad Little's Covid-19 Working Group, including the challenges of making decisions during the pandemic. He describes the process of gathering and analyzing data, the dynamics within the group, and the weight of responsibility felt in making recommendations. The speaker emphasizes the importance of data accuracy and the collaborative effort to provide the best guidance to the governor. He also touches on the complexities of balancing health concerns with the impact on education and businesses.

metadata.title: Experiences in the Covid-19 Working Group

He describes the process of gathering and analyzing data, the dynamics within the group, and the weight of responsibility felt in making recommendations. The speaker emphasizes the importance of data accuracy and the collaborative effort to provide the best guidance to the governor. He also touches on the complexities of balancing health concerns with the impact on education and businesses.

lines.line: # Handling of the Pandemic in Idaho

> The speaker reflects on the state's handling of the pandemic, starting from the initial response to the development of the Idaho rebound reopening plan. He discusses the challenges of interpreting data, the impact of decisions on various sectors, and the efforts to prevent healthcare system overload. The speaker also notes the differences in response between public and private schools and the importance of community support during the crisis.

metadata.title: Handling of the Pandemic in Idaho

lines.line: # Brian Mooney's Role as Director of Administration

> The speaker discusses his transition into the role of Director of Administration for the Idaho State Government, detailing how he was approached for the position and the responsibilities it entailed. He reflects on the skills he brought from the private sector to the public sector, such as managing healthcare contracts and procurement processes. The speaker also mentions his relationship with Governor Little, expressing that it strengthened during and after his tenure in the role.

metadata.title: Brian Mooney's Role as Director of Administration
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses his transition into the role of Director of Administration for the Idaho State Government, detailing how he was approached for the position and the responsibilities it entailed. He reflects on the skills he brought from the private sector to the public sector, such as managing healthcare contracts and procurement processes. The speaker also mentions his relationship with Governor Little, expressing that it strengthened during and after his tenure in the role.

lines.line: # Reflections on the State's Response Over the Years

> The speaker provides a year-by-year reflection on the state's response to the pandemic, from the initial denial and reactive measures in 2020 to the introduction of vaccines in 2021 and the continued challenges in 2022. He discusses the public's reaction to the pandemic, the political implications, and the importance of unity during crises. The speaker also touches on the cultural aspects of mask-wearing and the need for empathy and understanding in the face of judgment and criticism.

metadata.title: Reflections on the State's Response Over the Years

He discusses the public's reaction to the pandemic, the political implications, and the importance of unity during crises. The speaker also touches on the cultural aspects of mask-wearing and the need for empathy and understanding in the face of judgment and criticism.

lines.line: # Conclusion and Final Thoughts

> In the concluding part of the interview, the speaker is asked if there was anything that should have been asked but wasn't. He expresses his honor in being interviewed and hopes that the work of the Covid-19 Working Group will be beneficial for the state in the future. The state historian acknowledges the importance of preserving the records created by the group for future reference and historical research.

lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Interview

> The state historian for the Idaho State Historical Society, Hannah Laura Hein, begins the interview with Brian Mooney, Director of Operations at Capitol Distributing, by setting the context and date of the meeting. The purpose of the interview is to document Mooney's experiences as a member of Idaho Governor Brad Little's Covid-19 working group. Mooney provides his name and spelling, and the conversation starts with his background, including his upbringing in Idaho, education in Wyoming, and career progression that led him back to Idaho.

The purpose of the interview is to document Mooney's experiences as a member of Idaho Governor Brad Little's Covid-19 working group. Mooney provides his name and spelling, and the conversation starts with his background, including his upbringing in Idaho, education in Wyoming, and career progression that led him back to Idaho.

lines.line: # Experiences in the Covid-19 Working Group

> The speaker recounts his experiences as a member of the Covid-19 working group, including the challenges of interpreting data and making recommendations to the governor during the pandemic. He describes the collaborative nature of the group, the weight of the decisions they had to make, and the strategies employed to manage the crisis, such as hospital resource allocation and public health measures. The speaker also reflects on the balance between protecting public health and maintaining normalcy in education and business.

lines.line: # Brian Mooney's Role as Director of Administration

> The speaker discusses his tenure as the director of administration for the Idaho State Government, detailing the responsibilities of the role and how it prepared him for serving on the Covid-19 working group. He reflects on his relationship with Governor Little, both during and after his service in the administration, and his commitment to public service. The speaker also touches on the size and scope of the agency he led and the various tasks he managed, including overseeing healthcare contracts for state employees.

lines.line: # Reflecting on the State's Pandemic Response

> The speaker reflects on the state's response to the pandemic over the years, starting with the initial denial and subsequent reactive measures in 2020. He discusses the sense of security provided by vaccines in 2021 and the continued challenges of managing the pandemic, including public judgment and political division. The speaker emphasizes the importance of unity and learning from the crisis to improve future responses.

story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Bryan Mooney
story.description: The transcript is an interview with Bryan Mooney, exploring his role as a member of Idaho Governor Brad Little's COVID-19 Working Group. Topics include Mooney’s career, experiences during the COVID-19 pandemic, and efforts to manage the crisis. Mooney discusses initial disbelief about the virus, challenges of navigating a rapidly evolving situation, and the importance of data-driven decision-making. He reflects on the cautious approach of the working group and the complexities of balancing public health, economic impacts, and education. The discussion concludes with reflections on community efforts and the importance of historical documentation for future crisis management.
